title: nf-core/exogaptwo pipeline parameters
description: Annotation tool for eukaryotic non-model species

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to comma-separated file containing information about the samples in the experiment.
input.help_text: Path to a samplesheet CSV (header + 3 columns). See the usage docs for the required columns and examples: https://nf-co.re/exogaptwo/usage#samplesheet-input

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved.
outdir.help_text: Path to the directory where pipeline outputs will be written. Use absolute paths for cloud storage targets.

email.help_text: Set this to receive a summary email when the workflow finishes. If configured in your user Nextflow config (~/.nextflow/config) you don't need to pass it on each run.

comparative_genomics.description: Enable comparative genomics options for each activated module (requires at least 2 genomes).
comparative_genomics.help_text: Set to true to enable comparative genomics features. Requires at least two input genomes.

modules.title: Module to activate
modules.description: Define which module you want to use.

module_repeats.description: Activate the module for repetitive elements annotation.
module_repeats.help_text: Set to true to enable the repetitive elements annotation module. When enabled, fill in the module_repeats-related parameters.

module_genes.description: Activate the module for protein-coding genes annotation.
module_genes.help_text: Set to true to enable the protein-coding genes annotation module. When enabled, fill in the module_genes-related parameters.

module_ncgenes.description: Activate the module for non-coding genes annotation.
module_ncgenes.help_text: Set to true to enable the non-coding genes annotation module. When enabled, fill in the module_ncgenes-related parameters.

module_repeats_option.title: module_repeats options
module_repeats_option.description: Options for the module annotating repetitive elements.

download_famdb.help_text: Set to true to download a FamDB library from dfam.org. If false, provide a local path via famdb_path.

famdb_path.description: Path to the FamDB/dfam library in HDF5 format for repeat annotation.
famdb_path.help_text: Local path to a .h5 dfam/famdb file. If not provided, use famdb_to_download to fetch a library from dfam.org.

famdb_to_download.description: ID of a FamDB library to download from dfam.org (1-16).
famdb_to_download.help_text: If you don't provide a local famdb_path, set this to a string '1'..'16' to download the corresponding library from dfam.org. Leave null if you provide famdb_path locally.

group_consensus_sequences.description: Use the same consensus library for all genomes processed in the run.
group_consensus_sequences.help_text: If true, the pipeline will use one shared consensus sequence library across all input genomes; otherwise separate libraries may be used per genome.

famdb_to_download.description: ID of a FamDB library to download from dfam.org (1-16).

famdb_path.description: Path to a local FamDB .h5 file (required when download_famdb is false).

module_comparative_genomics_option.title: module_comparative_genomics options
module_comparative_genomics_option.description: Options for the optional module of comparative genomics.

comparative_genomics.description: Enable comparative genomics options for each activated module (requires at least 2 genomes).
comparative_genomics.help_text: Set to true to enable comparative genomics features. Requires at least two input genomes.

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: Centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a few pipeline parameters to describe themselves. These are printed to the Nextflow log and typically do not need editing.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id or branch for institutional configs.

custom_config_base.description: Base URL or directory for institutional config files.
custom_config_base.help_text: If running offline, download the institutional config files and point this parameter to their local location.

max_job_request_options.title: Max job request options
max_job_request_options.description: Set the top limit for requested resources for any single job.
max_job_request_options.help_text: These options cap the maximum resources any single process can request so pipelines run on smaller clusters. They cannot be used to increase a process's resource requests.

max_cpus.description: Maximum number of CPUs that can be requested for any single job.
max_cpus.help_text: Upper limit for CPU requests per process (integer). Example: --max_cpus 4

max_memory.description: Maximum amount of memory that can be requested for any single job.
max_memory.help_text: Upper limit for memory requests. Use Nextflow memory format, e.g. '8.GB'.

max_time.description: Maximum amount of time that can be requested for any single job.
max_time.help_text: Upper limit for walltime per process. Use format like '2.h' or '30.m'.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text: Common nf-core options to customise pipeline preferences. Typically set in ~/.nextflow/config.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: Controls how Nextflow's publishDir moves/saves files. Options: symlink, rellink, link, copy, copyNoFollow, move. See Nextflow docs: https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, sent only when the pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: Email address to receive a failure-only summary when the pipeline exits with an error.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.
plaintext_email.help_text: When true, summary emails are sent as plain text rather than HTML.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.
monochrome_logs.help_text: Disable coloured logging output to generate monochrome logs.

hook_url.description: Incoming hook URL for messaging service.
hook_url.help_text: Webhook URL for notifications (e.g. Slack or MS Teams). Leave unset if not using hooks.

validate_params.description: Validate runtime parameters against the schema.
validate_params.help_text: When true, the pipeline will validate provided parameters against this JSON schema at startup.

validationShowHiddenParams.help_text: By default, schema parameters marked as hidden are omitted from `--help`. Enable to show them.

validationFailUnrecognisedParams.description: Fail validation when an unrecognised parameter is found.
validationFailUnrecognisedParams.help_text: When true, the pipeline will error if unknown parameters are supplied. By default unknown parameters produce a warning.

validationLenientMode.help_text: Allows string values that can be parsed as numbers or booleans during validation. See the JSONSchema validator docs for details.
